CVE-2024-11003.title: Qualys discovered that needrestart, before version 3.8, passes un ...
CVE-2024-11003.description: Qualys discovered that needrestart, before version 3.8, passes unsanitized data to a library (Modules::ScanDeps) which expects safe input. This could allow a local attacker to execute arbitrary shell commands. Please see the related CVE-2024-10224 in Modules::ScanDeps.

CVE-2024-48990.title: Qualys discovered that needrestart, before version 3.8, allows lo ...
CVE-2024-48990.description: Qualys discovered that needrestart, before version 3.8, allows local attackers to execute arbitrary code as root by tricking needrestart into running the Python interpreter with an attacker-controlled PYTHONPATH environment variable.

CVE-2024-48991.title: Qualys discovered that needrestart, before version 3.8, allows lo ...
CVE-2024-48991.description: Qualys discovered that needrestart, before version 3.8, allows local attackers to execute arbitrary code as root by winning a race condition and tricking needrestart into running their own, fake Python interpreter (instead of the system's real Python interpreter). The initial security fix (6ce6136) introduced a regression which was subsequently resolved (42af5d3).

CVE-2024-48992.title: Qualys discovered that needrestart, before version 3.8, allows lo ...
CVE-2024-48992.description: Qualys discovered that needrestart, before version 3.8, allows local attackers to execute arbitrary code as root by tricking needrestart into running the Ruby interpreter with an attacker-controlled RUBYLIB environment variable.
